title: Aparine spuria
taxonomy: Aparine spuria is a species of plant that belongs to the family Polygonaceae. It is a type of bindweed, a group of plants that are known for their twining stems and small, white or pink flowers. Aparine spuria is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. The plant is an annual or biennial herb, meaning it completes its life cycle within one or two years. It is often found in dry, sandy areas and along roadsides, where it can grow quite vigorously.

The plant's extracts have been studied for their ability to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms and reduce inflammation. intro: Aparine spuria, also known as sticky willy or sticky catchweed, is a climbing or twining plant in the bryony family (Cucurbitaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which provide relief from wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Aparine spuria include cucurbitacins, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Aparine spuria is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat skin irritations, digestive issues, and as an anti-inflammatory agent.
